items.description: Vertical inscription 'urdur' (or 'urdir') written downwards in the outer margin beside the large initial.
items.scholarly_note: Possibly a pen trial, catchword fragment, or stray annotation; orientation and placement suggest it is not part of the main text flow.

items.description: Arabic numeral '11' written in the upper right corner.
items.scholarly_note: Modern foliation or pagination mark added post-medievally.

items.description: Small cross (+) mark placed in the outer margin.
items.scholarly_note: Likely a pen trial or simple reference mark.

items.description: Large irregular dark brown stain covering much of the lower text block and margin.
items.scholarly_note: Consistent with water or dirt damage; significantly darkens parchment and reduces local legibility.

items.region: lower left quadrant and scattered across lower third
items.description: Multiple smaller brown stains, blotches, and foxing spots.
Large irregular loss where the corner has been torn away, truncating the ends of the final lines of text.
items.scholarly_note: Text on the last 3–4 lines is incomplete due to the loss.

items.description: Heavily worn, ragged, and frayed lower margin with multiple small tears, losses, and abrasion.
items.scholarly_note: Parchment is thin, translucent, and mechanically weak along the edge.

items.region: lower left to center, crossing text lines
items.description: Faint linear surface abrasions running roughly horizontally and diagonally.
items.scholarly_note: Appear as lighter streaks; likely from handling, binding friction, or later cleaning.

items.description: Additional faint scratches and creases on the parchment surface.

scratches: faint horizontal and diagonal surface scratches across lower text block
light creasing and streaking visible under raking light
damage_notes: missing/torn bottom right corner with text loss
frayed and irregular bottom edge with multiple small losses
heavy brown staining concentrated in lower third
general edge wear, most severe at foot
parchment thinning and increased translucency in damaged zones
summary: Page 20 shows moderate to heavy damage focused at the foot: torn lower-right corner with text loss, extensive brown staining, and frayed bottom edge. A vertical marginal inscription 'urdur' and a small cross appear in the right margin. Evidence of corrections is minimal; surface scratches and handling marks are present but do not severely affect the main text block.
